snippets.summary: Thibault Sottiaux (thsottiaux on X) shared 3 recent updates. When we go from GPT-5.0 -> GPT-5.1 -> ... -> GPT-5.5, the number incrementing goes with improvements in capabilities and token efficiency (which translates to speed gains). With GPT-5.5 our best model yet. A simple strategy that we would like to continue. Five million users would agree. Resetting the limits tomorrow morning to celebrate.

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on coding and enterprise
snippets.takeaway: Again, maybe counterintuitive, but in the majority of conversations I have with CIOs, CTOs, and CEOs in large enterprises, they are either growing due to AI (in new job functions...
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 1 recent update. Again, maybe counterintuitive, but in the majority of conversations I have with CIOs, CTOs, and CEOs in large enterprises, they are either growing due to AI (in new job functions like FDEs, engineering, etc.) or at a minimum reinvesting efficiency savings back into the business in new areas (sales, marketing, etc.). David Solomon, CEO of Goldman Sachs, articulated this perfectly in a NYTimes OpEd last week.

snippets.title: Peter Steinberger on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: With GPT 5.5, /goal, autoreview and crabbox my prompts moved from ~30-60min to often 4-10h tasks and my confidence that it’s ready is much much higher. Yielding agents is a skill.
snippets.summary: Peter Steinberger (steipete on X) shared 3 recent updates. With GPT 5.5, /goal, autoreview and crabbox my prompts moved from ~30-60min to often 4-10h tasks and my confidence that it’s ready is much much higher. Yielding agents is a skill. I do this with codex all the time. Ask it to review code for bugs and it will tell you all good, tell it there is a bug and it will LOOP AND LOOP and will find issues. Autoreview: crabbox:

snippets.title: New in Claude Managed Agents: dreaming, outcomes, and multiagent orchestration
snippets.takeaway: New in Claude Managed Agents: dreaming, outcomes, and multiagent orchestration. Today we're launching dreaming in Claude Managed Agents as a research preview.
snippets.summary: New in Claude Managed Agents: dreaming, outcomes, and multiagent orchestration. Today we're launching dreaming in Claude Managed Agents as a research preview. Dreaming extends memory by reviewing past sessions to find patterns and help agents self-improve. We're also making outcomes, multiagent orchestration, and webhooks available to developers building with Managed Agents.

snippets.title: Boris Cherny on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: Salesforce published a detailed writeup on going agentic with Claude Code. A couple things jumped out. A migration they'd scoped at 231 days shipped in 13.
snippets.summary: Boris Cherny (bcherny on X) shared 3 recent updates. Salesforce published a detailed writeup on going agentic with Claude Code. A couple things jumped out. A migration they'd scoped at 231 days shipped in 13. One PR delivered 21 endpoints at 100% test coverage. The teams seeing the biggest wins from AI are completely changing how they work, not speeding up what they already do. What steps can you delete, what handoffs go away, what can an agent just own end to end.

snippets.title: Building an AI Guardian for Enterprise with Onyx Security CEO Maxim Bar Kogan
snippets.takeaway: Enterprise AI is moving from chat rollout toward agentic workflows, with token budgets, rollout speed, and workflow integration becoming the practical bottlenecks.
snippets.summary: And we've seen some of that happen lately with agents accidentally publishing code and tokens that they weren't supposed to. Like, definitely, enterprises are starting to realize that risk has grown exponentially and that they don't have any way to stop the adoption. And we've seen some of that happen lately with downtimes that were caused by agents doing their own thing, agents accidentally publishing code and tokens that they weren't supposed to, and so on.

snippets.sourceName: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
snippets.title: State of Enterprise AI 2026: Aaron Levie on Tokenmaxxing, Rise of Headless, and AI-Proofing Your Job
snippets.takeaway: Enterprise AI is moving from chat rollout toward agentic workflows, with token budgets, rollout speed, and workflow integration becoming the practical bottlenecks.
snippets.summary: Aaron is hands down one of the most deeply thoughtful CEOs in tech today when it comes to agentic AI, and he has a front row seat to how AI is actually being deployed inside the world's largest enterprises. In this episode, we get into the most pressing topics in enterprise AI right now, including soaring token costs, why AI model progress is paradoxically slowing down enterprise deployment, the rise of headless software, the emergence of internal FDEs, and where startups can still win.
entities: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck

snippets.title: Swyx on Codex and agentic spreadsheets
snippets.takeaway: Swyx called Codex almost unrecognizable from three months earlier and framed the new demo as agentic Excel on Mac.
snippets.summary: Swyx highlighted how quickly Codex appears to be evolving, pointing to a demo from Gabriel Chua as evidence that the product is moving toward more agentic, spreadsheet-like work on Mac. The interesting signal is not just one feature, but the pace of product iteration around practical AI work surfaces.

snippets.title: Swyx on national AI agent infrastructure
snippets.takeaway: A Singapore AI GovTech estimate points to 1.3 billion agents in the country within two years, with a national MCP gateway under construction.
snippets.summary: The post is a useful signal for anyone watching AI infrastructure move from individual tools to public-sector platforms. The emphasis on a national MCP gateway suggests governments may treat agent interoperability as shared infrastructure rather than scattered app-by-app integration.
